description: Validated reference positions used by behavioral-evals.json. Tolerances in the assertions account for small differences in implementation (e.g., true vs apparent positions, Newcomb vs Newcomb-revised obliquity). The reference subjects ('Alex' and 'Jordan') are fictional test personas with synthetic birth data, chosen to exercise historical-DST resolution (US Central/Mountain zones) the same way the suite always has.

source_schema._doc: Each chart section may declare a _default_source. Any individual entry that deviates from the default is expressed as {"value": ..., "source": ...} so the provenance of every reference value is explicit. This convention exists so that a change in one ephemeris source can be traced to exactly the entries it affects, rather than silently shifting values whose provenance was ambiguous.

codes.swisseph_builtin: Swiss Ephemeris via pyswisseph 2.10 with Moshier fallback (works without external files). Accuracy: arcsecond-class for major planets, asteroids covered by Swiss Eph built-in.
codes.swisseph_with_seas18: Swiss Ephemeris with the asteroid file seas_18.se1 installed (covers Chiron, Ceres, Pallas, Juno, Vesta with arcsecond/arcminute accuracy 1800-2100). As of skill v1.2 this file is bundled in <skill>/ephe/ and is the required best source for these bodies.
codes.keplerian_jpl_j2000: JPL Small-Body Database J2000 osculating Keplerian elements solved via the skill's lib/kepler.py. ±arcminute for Ceres/Pallas/Juno/Vesta/Eris; ±1-2° for Chiron over multi-decade ranges due to Saturn perturbations.
codes.keplerian_spec_v1: Keplerian elements as documented in the original design spec §8 v1. A Chiron M₀ error in that table (a JPL Horizons query mislabeled as J2000) produces a wrong Chiron position; use keplerian_jpl_j2000 instead. Other bodies' v1 elements are within tolerance.
codes.derived: Computed from other values in this file (midpoints, Davison moment, etc.).
codes.manual: Hand-entered or cross-checked by a human (typically birth data or interpretive flags).

computation_notes.ephemeris: Swiss Ephemeris built-in (Moshier fallback for missing files)
computation_notes.asteroid_method: Swiss Ephemeris with bundled seas_18.se1 for Chiron/Ceres; JPL Keplerian elements for Eris (no bundled Swiss Eph file for asteroid 136199)
computation_notes.time_method: All times converted to UT via IANA zoneinfo before computation
computation_notes.chiron_note: Chiron is computed via Swiss Ephemeris with the bundled seas_18.se1 file (arcsecond accuracy 1800-2100), NOT via the Keplerian fallback. The Keplerian path carries ±1-2° error for Chiron over multi-decade ranges (Saturn perturbations), which is large enough to flip close synastry contacts; the bundled-Swiss-Eph value is the truth source for all Chiron reference entries here.
computation_notes.ceres_note: Ceres is likewise computed via swisseph_with_seas18. The Keplerian Ceres element set carries larger error than its nominal 'arcminute' claim suggests, so any Ceres-dependent reference contact must be derived from the Swiss-Eph value, not the Keplerian fallback.

Chiron.notes: Computed from seas_18.se1 (bundled). The Keplerian fallback would carry ±1-2° here.

Eris.notes: Eris has no bundled Swiss Eph file (no s136199s.se1 without an extra download), so the Keplerian fallback remains best-source for Eris.

vedic_lahiri_natal.Sun: 0° Gemini 57' (Mithuna) — about 23.7° earlier than the tropical position
vedic_lahiri_natal.Moon: 14° Gemini 47' (Mithuna) — nakshatra Ardra pada 3, lord Rahu
vedic_lahiri_natal.Lagna: 9° Cancer 00' (Karka) — the sidereal rising sign is Cancer, NOT Leo as in the tropical chart

vimshottari_dasha_current.birth_mahadasha: Rahu (Moon in Ardra; ~61% of the Rahu period elapsed at birth)

solar_return_2026.moment_ut: 2026-06-15T17:50Z (± 5 minutes; exact moment the Sun returns to 24°39' Gemini)

Eris.notes: Eris remains Keplerian (no bundled Swiss Eph file for asteroid 136199).

tropical_features_notable: Uranus-Neptune approaching their early-1990s Capricorn conjunction

synastry_alex_jordan._chiron_note: Chiron contacts are derived against Swiss-Eph Chiron values (Alex 29° Gemini 27', Jordan 22° Cancer 32' R). Any Chiron contact computed via the Keplerian fallback would shift by up to ±1-2° and could form or break spuriously; only the Swiss-Eph-derived contacts are correct.

composite_alex_jordan_midpoint._convention_note: Spec §16 mandates the shorter-arc midpoint rule; the implementation in <skill>/scripts/composite.py uses shorter-arc. Houses are equal-house from the composite Ascendant.

moment.place: Temporal+spatial midpoint of Chicago and Denver (eastern Nebraska)
